name: Domovoi Extended
description: The Domovoi is a household spirit in Slavic folklore, an embodiment of the ancestors and protector of the home. This extended entry delves deeper into the Domovoi's multifaceted nature. More than just a guardian, the Domovoi's mood directly impacts the household's fortune. A well-treated Domovoi ensures prosperity, health, and harmony, while a neglected or angered one can bring misfortune, illness, and even poltergeist-like activity. He is said to be able to predict future events, often warning of impending danger through subtle signs like knocking or breaking objects. Appeasing the Domovoi involves leaving him small offerings of food, such as bread, salt, or porridge, and treating the house with respect. Ignoring him, failing to maintain a clean home, or engaging in disruptive behavior can easily provoke his ire. He is particularly sensitive to swearing and loud arguments. The Domovoi's relationship with the family is symbiotic; he protects them, and in return, they respect and appease him. Some believe each house has its own Domovoi, passed down through generations, while others believe a new one attaches itself to each new home. The Domovoi is not inherently good or evil, but rather a reflection of the household's own character. A loving, respectful family will be blessed with a helpful Domovoi, while a neglectful or cruel family will suffer his wrath. He is a complex and nuanced figure, representing the deep connection between the family and their home, a tangible manifestation of their history and destiny.
subtitle: Guardian spirit of the home.
appearance: The Domovoi's appearance varies. He is often described as a small, old man with a long beard, covered in fur or hair. He can also appear as a shadow or a small animal, such as a cat or dog. Some say he resembles the oldest male member of the family, or a deceased ancestor. He is rarely seen clearly, often glimpsed out of the corner of one's eye.
habitat: He lives in the home, usually near the stove, the threshold, or in the attic. He is strongly tied to the house itself and rarely leaves.
behavior: The Domovoi is generally benevolent if treated well. He helps with chores, protects the home from danger, and warns of impending problems. If angered, he can become mischievous, hiding objects, making noises, or even causing physical harm. He is fiercely protective of his family and will defend them against outsiders.

keyMyths.title: The Moving of the Domovoi
keyMyths.description: When a family moves to a new home, it is important to invite the Domovoi to come along. Failure to do so can result in misfortune in the new house. The invitation is typically done through a special ritual and offering.

keyMyths.title: Appeasing the Domovoi
keyMyths.description: To keep the Domovoi happy, families leave offerings of food and drink, such as bread, salt, porridge, or milk. These offerings are typically placed near the stove or in a corner of the house. A happy Domovoi brings good fortune.
